articles.title: Smoked cannabis reduces immediate alcohol consumption in controlled laboratory trial
articles.titleKo: 스모킹 대마초가 통제된 실험실 환경에서 즉각적인 알코올 섭취를 감소시킨다
articles.summary: In a controlled laboratory setting, heavy drinkers consumed up to 27 percent less alcohol after smoking cannabis. The findings offer new insights into how acute cannabis intoxication alters immediate drinking habits and delays the onset of alcohol consumption.
articles.aiSummary: This study examined the immediate effects of cannabis on alcohol consumption among heavy drinkers. Conducted in a controlled laboratory setting, researchers compared alcohol consumption before and after cannabis smoking in a group of participants with heavy drinking habits. The key finding showed that participants who smoked cannabis consumed up to 27% less alcohol compared to those who did not, and also delayed the onset of drinking. This research provides new insights into how acute cannabis intoxication alters immediate drinking patterns and postpones alcohol consumption initiation. However, the study was conducted only in a controlled laboratory environment, making it unclear whether these results would translate to real-world settings. Additionally, the research was limited to heavy drinkers, restricting generalizability to the broader population.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 이 연구는 대마초가 음주량에 미치는 즉각적인 영향을 조사하기 위해 진행되었습니다. 통제된 실험실 환경에서 헤비 드링커(과음 습관이 있는 사람들)들을 대상으로 대마초 흡연 전후의 알코올 소비량을 비교하는 실험이 실시되었습니다. 연구 결과, 대마초를 피운 참가자들은 피우지 않은 참가자들에 비해 최대 27% 적게 알코올을 소비했으며, 음주를 시작하는 시간도 지연되는 것으로 나타났습니다. 이 발견은 급성 대마초 중독 상태가 즉각적인 음주 습관을 어떻게 변화시키는지에 대한 새로운 이해를 제공합니다. 다만 이 연구는 통제된 실험실 환경에서만 진행되었으므로 실제 일상생활에서의 결과가 동일하게 나타날지는 불명확한 한계가 있습니다. 또한 연구 대상이 헤비 드링커에 한정되어 있어 일반 인구집단에 일반화하기 어렵습니다.

The key finding showed that participants who smoked cannabis consumed up to 27% less alcohol compared to those who did not, and also delayed the onset of drinking. This research provides new insights into how acute cannabis intoxication alters immediate drinking patterns and postpones alcohol consumption initiation. However, the study was conducted only in a controlled laboratory environment, making it unclear whether these results would translate to real-world settings. Additionally, the research was limited to heavy drinkers, restricting generalizability to the broader population.
keyPoints: **정량적 효과**: 대마초 흡연 후 알코올 소비량이 최대 27% 감소 / Cannabis smoking resulted in up to 27% reduction in alcohol consumption
**음주 개시 지연**: 대마초 사용이 음주 시작 시간을 지연시킴 / Cannabis intoxication delays the onset of alcohol consumption
**연구 설계의 강점**: 통제된 실험실 환경에서 정확한 측정 가능 / Controlled laboratory setting allows for precise measurement and causality establishment

articles.title: How Emotion Shapes the Behavior of LLMs and Agents: A Mechanistic Study
articles.titleKo: LLM 및 에이전트의 행동을 형성하는 감정: 메커니즘 연구
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emotion plays an important role in human cognition and performance. Motivated by this, we investigate whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and agents. Existing emotion-aware studies mainly treat emotion as a surface-level style factor or a perception target, overlooking its mechanistic role in task processing. To address this limitation, we propose E-STEER, an interpretable emotion steering framework that enables direct representation-level intervention in LLMs and agents. It embeds emotion as
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and AI agents, motivated by emotion's important role in human cognition and performance. While existing emotion-aware studies treat emotion merely as a surface-level style factor or perception target, this work examines emotion's mechanistic role in task processing. The researchers propose E-STEER, an interpretable emotion steering framework that enables direct representation-level intervention in LLMs and agents, embedding emotion as a fundamental component in cognitive processing. The significance of this work lies in providing more fine-grained control methods for AI system behavior and elucidating how emotions mechanistically influence AI decision-making processes at a fundamental level. However, the study is in its early stages, requiring validation across diverse LLM models and real-world application scenarios, as well as further discussion on how emotional representations should be defined and measured.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 인간의 인지와 행동에서 감정이 중요한 역할을 한다는 점에 착안하여, 대형언어모델(LLM)과 AI 에이전트도 유사한 감정 신호에 의해 행동이 변할 수 있는지를 조사했습니다. 기존 감정 관련 연구들은 감정을 표면적인 스타일 요소나 인식 대상으로만 다루었으나, 본 연구는 감정이 과제 처리 과정에서 근본적인 역할을 한다고 가정합니다. 이를 검증하기 위해 연구팀은 'E-STEER'라는 해석 가능한 감정 조종 프레임워크를 제안하였으며, 이는 LLM과 AI 에이전트의 표현 수준에 직접 개입할 수 있습니다. 본 연구의 주요 의의는 AI 시스템의 행동을 더욱 세밀하게 제어할 수 있는 방법을 제시하고, 감정이 AI의 의사결정 과정에 어떻게 영향을 미치는지를 메커니즘 차원에서 규명했다는 점입니다. 다만 본 연구는 아직 초기 단계로, 다양한 LLM 모델과 실제 응용 환경에서의 검증이 필요하며, 감정 표현의 정의와 측정 방식에 대한 추가적 논의가 요구됩니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigates whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and AI agents, motivated by emotion's important role in human cognition and performance. While existing emotion-aware studies treat emotion merely as a surface-level style factor or perception target, this work examines emotion's mechanistic role in task processing. The researchers propose E-STEER, an interpretable emotion steering framework that enables direct representation-level intervention in LLMs and agents, embedding emotion as a fundamental component in cognitive processing. The significance of this work lies in providing more fine-grained control methods for AI system behavior and elucidating how emotions mechanistically influence AI decision-making processes at a fundamental level. However, the study is in its early stages, requiring validation across diverse LLM models and real-world application scenarios, as well as further discussion on how emotional representations should be defined and measured.

##
keyPoints: **감정의 역할 재정의** / **Redefining Emotion's Role**: 기존의 감정을 표면적 스타일로만 보는 관점에서 벗어나, 과제 처리 과정의 근본적 메커니즘으로 감정을 위치지음
**E-STEER 프레임워크** / **E-STEER Framework**: 표현 수준에서 직접 개입이 가능한 해석 가능한 감정 조종 방식으로, 기존 방식보다 더 정교한 AI 제어가 가능
**AI 행동 제어의 고도화** / **Advanced AI Behavior Control**: 감정 신호를 통해 LLM과 에이전트의 행동을 메커니즘 차원에서 조종할 수 있게 함으로써 AI 제어의 새로운 가능성 제시
**향후 검증 필요** / **Need for Further Validation**: 다양한 모델과 실제 응용 환경에서의 광범위한 검증이 필요하며, 감정 표현의 정의와 측정 방식에 대한 학제 간 논의 요구

articles.title: One Panel Does Not Fit All: Case-Adaptive Multi-Agent Deliberation for Clinical Prediction
articles.titleKo: 하나의 패널로는 부족하다: 임상 예측을 위한 사례 적응형 다중 에이전트 심의
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models applied to clinical prediction exhibit case-level heterogeneity: simple cases yield consistent outputs, while complex cases produce divergent predictions under minor prompt changes. Existing single-agent strategies sample from one role-conditioned distribution, and multi-agent frameworks use fixed roles with flat majority voting, discarding the diagnostic signal in disagreement. We propose CAMP (Case-Adaptive Multi-agent Panel), where an attending-physician agent dynamically assembles a specialist panel tailored to each case
articles.aiSummary:
Large language models applied to clinical prediction show case-level heterogeneity, where simple cases produce consistent outputs but complex cases exhibit divergent predictions with minor prompt variations. Existing single-agent approaches sample from one role-conditioned distribution, while current multi-agent frameworks use fixed roles with flat majority voting, failing to leverage the diagnostic signal contained in disagreement among agents. This study proposes CAMP (Case-Adaptive Multi-agent Panel), a system where an attending-physician agent dynamically assembles a specialized panel tailored to each individual case's complexity. This adaptive approach shows potential to improve both accuracy and consistency in clinical prediction tasks. However, the research is limited by the need for evaluation on real clinical datasets and validation across diverse medical domains to confirm effectiveness.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
의료 AI 분야에서 대규모 언어모델(LLM)을 임상 예측에 적용할 때, 단순한 사례는 일관된 결과를 보이지만 복잡한 사례는 사소한 프롬프트 변화에도 예측 결과가 크게 달라지는 문제가 발생한다. 기존의 단일 에이전트 방식이나 고정된 역할을 가진 다중 에이전트 시스템은 의사들의 의견 불일치 속에 담긴 진단 신호를 제대로 활용하지 못하고 있다. 이 연구에서는 CAMP(Case-Adaptive Multi-agent Panel)라는 새로운 시스템을 제안하는데, 담당의 역할을 하는 AI 에이전트가 각 사례의 복잡도에 따라 적절한 전문가 패널을 동적으로 구성하는 방식이다. 이러한 적응형 접근방식은 임상 예측의 정확성과 일관성을 향상시킬 수 있는 가능성을 보여준다. 다만 실제 임상 데이터에 대한 평가가 이루어져야 하며, 다양한 의료 분야에서의 효과성 검증이 필요하다는 한계가 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Large language models applied to clinical prediction show case-level heterogeneity, where simple cases produce consistent outputs but complex cases exhibit divergent predictions with minor prompt variations. Existing single-agent approaches sample from one role-conditioned distribution, while current multi-agent frameworks use fixed roles with flat majority voting, failing to leverage the diagnostic signal contained in disagreement among agents. This study proposes CAMP (Case-Adaptive Multi-agent Panel), a system where an attending-physician agent dynamically assembles a specialized panel tailored to each individual case's complexity. This adaptive approach shows potential to improve both accuracy and consistency in clinical prediction tasks. However, the research is limited by the need for evaluation on real clinical datasets and validation across diverse medical domains to confirm effectiveness.

##
keyPoints: **문제 인식**: 임상 AI에서 사례 난이도에 따른 예측 불일치 문제 / Problem identification: Case-level prediction inconsistency in clinical AI systems based on case complexity
**혁신적 접근**: 담당의 에이전트가 각 사례에 맞춰 동적으로 전문가 패널 구성 / Innovative approach: Dynamic specialist panel assembly tailored to each case by attending-physician agent
**의견 불일치의 가치 활용**: 기존의 단순 다수결 투표에서 벗어나 불일치 속 진단 신호 추출 / Leveraging disagreement signals: Moving beyond flat majority voting to extract diagnostic value from agent disagreement
**임상 적용 가능성**: 복잡한 의료 사례의 예측 정확성 향상을 위한 실무적 해결책 / Clinical applicability: Practical solution for improving prediction accuracy in complex medical cases

articles.title: Open, Reliable, and Collective: A Community-Driven Framework for Tool-Using AI Agents
articles.titleKo: 개방적이고 신뢰할 수 있으며 협력적인: 도구 활용 AI 에이전트를 위한 커뮤니티 주도 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00137v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-integrated LLMs can retrieve, compute, and take real-world actions via external tools, but reliability remains a key bottleneck. We argue that failures stem from both tool-use accuracy (how well an agent invokes a tool) and intrinsic tool accuracy (the tool's own correctness), while most prior work emphasizes the former. We introduce OpenTools, a community-driven toolbox that standardizes tool schemas, provides lightweight plug-and-play wrappers, and evaluates tools with automated test suites and continuous monitoring. We also release a publ
articles.aiSummary:
Current AI systems that integrate large language models (LLMs) with external tools face reliability challenges due to poor tool-use performance. While previous research has focused mainly on how AI agents invoke tools incorrectly, this study argues that intrinsic tool failures are equally important contributors to system failures. To address this issue, the research team introduces OpenTools, a community-driven toolbox framework that standardizes tool schemas and provides lightweight plug-and-play integration wrappers. The framework includes automated test suites and continuous monitoring systems to evaluate tool accuracy and performance. OpenTools enables collaborative development where multiple contributors can improve and validate tools together through standardized processes. This research makes a significant contribution to enhancing the reliability of AI-integrated systems. However, the framework's success depends on achieving sufficient community participation and ensuring diverse tool coverage.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현재 대형언어모델(LLM)에 외부 도구를 연결한 AI 시스템들은 도구 사용 능력이 떨어져 신뢰성이 낮다는 문제가 있습니다. 기존 연구들은 AI가 도구를 잘못 사용하는 문제에만 집중했지만, 이 연구팀은 도구 자체의 결함도 중요한 원인이라고 주장합니다. 이를 해결하기 위해 연구팀은 'OpenTools'라는 커뮤니티 기반 도구 모음을 개발했습니다. OpenTools는 도구의 기능을 표준화하고, 플러그 앤 플레이 방식의 간편한 연결 기능을 제공하며, 자동화된 테스트와 지속적인 모니터링으로 도구의 정확성을 평가합니다. 이 프레임워크는 여러 개발자가 함께 도구를 개선하고 검증할 수 있는 구조를 제공합니다. 본 연구는 AI 시스템의 신뢰성을 높이는 데 의미 있는 기여를 할 것으로 예상됩니다. 다만 커뮤니티 참여도 및 도구의 다양성 확보가 성공의 주요 과제입니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:

The framework includes automated test suites and continuous monitoring systems to evaluate tool accuracy and performance. OpenTools enables collaborative development where multiple contributors can improve and validate tools together through standardized processes. This research makes a significant contribution to enhancing the reliability of AI-integrated systems. However, the framework's success depends on achieving sufficient community participation and ensuring diverse tool coverage.

##
keyPoints: **도구 신뢰성의 이중 문제** / Dual reliability problem: 도구 사용 능력뿐만 아니라 도구 자체의 정확성도 중요 / Both how agents use tools and the tools' own correctness matter equally
**표준화된 도구 스키마** / Standardized tool schemas: 플러그 앤 플레이 방식으로 도구를 쉽게 연결하고 호환성을 보장 / Enabling easy integration and compatibility through lightweight wrappers
**자동화된 검증 시스템** / Automated validation: 자동화된 테스트 스위트와 지속적 모니터링으로 도구의 정확성을 체계적으로 평가 / Systematic evaluation through automated test suites and continuous monitoring
**커뮤니티 기반 개발** / Community-driven approach: 개방형 프레임워크를 통해 여러 개발자가 협력하여 도구를 개선하는 생태계 구축 / Building an ecosystem where multiple developers collaboratively improve and validate tools

articles.title: A Safety-Aware Role-Orchestrated Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Behavioral Health Communication Simulation
articles.titleKo: 행동 건강 커뮤니케이션 시뮬레이션을 위한 안전-인식형 역할-조율 다중에이전트 LLM 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Single-agent large language model (LLM) systems struggle to simultaneously support diverse conversational functions and maintain safety in behavioral health communication. We propose a safety-aware, role-orchestrated multi-agent LLM framework designed to simulate supportive behavioral health dialogue through coordinated, role-differentiated agents. Conversational responsibilities are decomposed across specialized agents, including empathy-focused, action-oriented, and supervisory roles, while a prompt-based controller dynamically activates releva
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the challenge that single-agent large language models (LLMs) struggle to simultaneously support diverse conversational functions while maintaining safety in behavioral health communication. The research team proposes a safety-aware, role-orchestrated multi-agent LLM framework where specialized AI agents with differentiated roles—including empathy-focused, action-oriented, and supervisory functions—work collaboratively. A prompt-based controller dynamically activates relevant agents depending on the conversational context and needs. The innovation lies in prioritizing safety mechanisms in mental health counseling rather than merely enhancing dialogue quality, with potential applications in crisis intervention and therapeutic support in clinical settings. The framework demonstrates how modular agent design can decompose complex conversational responsibilities while maintaining ethical safeguards. However, as a preliminary study, it requires validation with real clinical data and user satisfaction evaluations in future research.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

##

keyPoints: **다중에이전트 설계의 장점** / Advantages of Multi-Agent Architecture

articles.title: Two-Stage Optimizer-Aware Online Data Selection for Large Language Models
articles.titleKo: 대규모 언어 모델을 위한 이단계 최적화기 인식 온라인 데이터 선택
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00001v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Gradient-based data selection offers a principled framework for estimating sample utility in large language model (LLM) fine-tuning, but existing methods are mostly designed for offline settings. They are therefore less suited to online fine-tuning, where data arrives sequentially, sample utility is step-dependent, and the effective update geometry is shaped by adaptive optimizers. articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the challenge of efficiently selecting sequential data during fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs) in online settings. Existing offline data selection methods assume pre-collected datasets, but real-world online environments present sequential data arrival where sample utility varies at each step. The research team proposes an optimizer-aware framework for gradient-based online data selection and reweighting in LLM fine-tuning. This method considers the characteristics of adaptive optimizers to determine which data most effectively improves model performance. Through a two-stage structure, it performs data selection and weight adjustment simultaneously, enhancing fine-tuning efficiency. This approach is expected to be valuable in practical applications by reducing computational costs while maintaining model performance. However, additional validation across various LLM scales and in real online environments is needed.

articles.titleKo: 작업 중심의 개인화된 페더레이션 언어 모델 미세조정
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00050v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a promising technique for training language models on distributed and private datasets of diverse tasks. However, aggregating models trained on heterogeneous tasks often degrades the overall performance of individual clients. To address this issue, Personalized FL (pFL) aims to create models tailored for each client's data distribution. Although these approaches improve local performance, they usually lack robustness in two aspects: (i) generalization: when clients must make predictions on unseen tasks, or f
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the limitations of Federated Learning (FL) when training language models on distributed and private datasets with heterogeneous tasks. Traditional FL faces performance degradation when aggregating models trained on diverse tasks across different clients. The researchers developed a Personalized FL (pFL) approach to create task-specific models tailored to each client's data distribution. However, existing pFL methods lack robustness in generalization when clients encounter unseen tasks and in overall model stability. The study's core contribution is implementing a task-centric personalization technique to simultaneously improve model stability and performance across diverse environments. This research demonstrates the feasibility of privacy-preserving efficient language model training in distributed settings. However, the study remains in its preliminary stage and requires additional validation for large-scale practical applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Vulnerable narcissism is linked to intense celebrity worship via parasocial relationships
articles.titleKo: 취약성 자기애는 준사회적 관계를 통한 강렬한 셀러브리티 숭배와 연관되어 있다
articles.summary: Scientists have found that individuals with vulnerable narcissism tend to form deep, unreciprocated relationships with famous figures. This psychological coping strategy helps explain why these insecure individuals face a higher risk for extreme celebrity worship.
articles.aiSummary:
This study examined the relationship between vulnerable narcissism and extreme celebrity worship by investigating how individuals with narcissistic traits form one-sided parasocial relationships with famous figures. The research focuses on understanding why people with internal insecurity and low self-esteem pursue deep emotional connections with celebrities. The key finding reveals that parasocial relationships serve as a psychological coping mechanism for individuals seeking emotional stability and self-validation. These unreciprocated connections with famous personalities can escalate into pathological celebrity worship behaviors. The significance of this research lies in illuminating the underlying psychological mechanisms behind obsessive fandom and fan behavior. However, the study may be limited by the lack of specific quantitative data regarding the formation process of parasocial relationships and the generalizability of findings across different populations.

##

articles.title: Brain scans reveal the neural fingerprints of dark personality traits
articles.titleKo: 뇌 스캔으로 드러난 검은 성격 특성의 신경학적 지문
articles.summary: Brain scans reveal that people with dark personality traits show heightened resting activity in goal-directed networks and dampened activity in empathy centers. This neurobiological mismatch suggests their brains are constantly primed to strategize rather than connect with others.
articles.aiSummary:
A new neurobiological study examined brain activity patterns in individuals with dark personality traits (narcissism, psychopathy, etc.) using brain imaging technology. Researchers compared brain scans of participants with dark personality traits to a control group, measuring resting-state neural activity across different brain regions. Key findings revealed that individuals with dark traits showed heightened activity in goal-directed strategic networks but significantly dampened activity in empathy and social connection centers. This neurobiological mismatch indicates their brains are intrinsically wired to prioritize strategic goal-pursuit over emotional connection with others. The research provides valuable insight into the neurobiological foundations of personality disorders and may inform future therapeutic interventions. However, limitations include relatively small sample sizes and the inability to establish clear causal relationships between neural patterns and behavioral outcomes.

##

articles.title: Evolution Strategies for Deep RL pretraining
articles.titleKo: 심화 강화학습 사전학습을 위한 진화 전략
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00066v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although Deep Reinforcement Learning has proven highly effective for complex decision-making problems, it demands significant computational resources and careful parameter adjustment in order to develop successful strategies. Evolution strategies offer a more straightforward, derivative-free approach that is less computationally costly and simpler to deploy. However, ES generally do not match the performance levels achieved by DRL, which calls into question their suitability for more demanding scenarios. This study examines the performance of ES
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the computational demands and parameter-tuning complexity of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) in solving complex decision-making problems. The researchers propose Evolution Strategies (ES) as an alternative approach that requires fewer computational resources and is easier to implement since it uses a derivative-free method. However, ES typically underperforms compared to DRL, raising questions about its suitability for challenging tasks. The research systematically examines ES performance during the pretraining phase to explore possibilities of combining the advantages of both approaches. This investigation offers significant implications for improving both efficiency and practicality of AI systems. Notably, the research is in early stages and requires validation of performance in real-world applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.aiSummary: This study addresses the "continual learning" challenge where AI agents must learn new experiences while avoiding catastrophic forgetting under fixed memory constraints. Rather than using traditional fixed parameter vectors, the research team proposes an innovative framework that treats memory as a stochastic process utilizing "Bridge Diffusion" mathematics, encoding present information at the terminal marginal and past information at intermediate marginals. A three-step "Compress-Add-Smooth (CAS)" recursive algorithm was developed to integrate new experiences efficiently. The approach demonstrates significant improvements in memory efficiency compared to existing methods, though the computational complexity of stochastic process-based calculations may present practical implementation challenges. This research represents an important theoretical contribution to enabling continuous learning in AI systems operating under resource-constrained environments.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 본 연구는 제한된 메모리 환경에서 인공지능 에이전트가 새로운 경험을 학습하면서도 과거의 경험을 잊지 않아야 하는 '연속 학습' 문제를 다룹니다. 연구팀은 기존의 고정된 매개변수 벡터 방식 대신 확률 과정(stochastic process)을 메모리로 사용하는 혁신적인 프레임워크를 제안했습니다. 이 방식은 '브릿지 확산(Bridge Diffusion)'이라는 수학적 기법을 활용하여 현재 정보는 종단점에, 과거 정보는 중간 지점에 인코딩합니다. 새로운 경험을 통합하기 위해 '압축-추가-평활(Compress-Add-Smooth, CAS)' 3단계 반복 알고리즘을 개발했습니다. 이 접근방식은 기존 방법 대비 메모리 효율성을 크게 향상시킬 수 있으나, 확률 과정 기반의 복잡한 계산 비용이 실제 적용에서의 제약이 될 수 있습니다. 연구는 제한된 자원 환경에서 AI 시스템의 지속적 학습 가능성을 보여주는 중요한 이론적 기여입니다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: This study addresses the "continual learning" challenge where AI agents must learn new experiences while avoiding catastrophic forgetting under fixed memory constraints. Rather than using traditional fixed parameter vectors, the research team proposes an innovative framework that treats memory as a stochastic process utilizing "Bridge Diffusion" mathematics, encoding present information at the terminal marginal and past information at intermediate marginals. A three-step "Compress-Add-Smooth (CAS)" recursive algorithm was developed to integrate new experiences efficiently. The approach demonstrates significant improvements in memory efficiency compared to existing methods, though the computational complexity of stochastic process-based calculations may present practical implementation challenges. This research represents an important theoretical contribution to enabling continuous learning in AI systems operating under resource-constrained environments.
keyPoints: **연속 학습의 메모리 문제 해결** / Addressing Memory Trade-offs in Continual Learning: 제한된 메모리 예산 내에서 새로운 경험과 기존 경험의 균형을 맞추는 핵심 문제를 혁신적으로 해결
**확률 과정 기반 메모리 표현** / Stochastic Process-Based Memory Representation: 기존 고정 벡터 대신 확률 과정(브릿지 확산)을 메모리로 활용하여 시간에 따른 정보 계층화 구현
**CAS 알고리즘의 효율성** / Efficiency of Compress-Add-Smooth Algorithm: 3단계 재귀 과정을 통해 메모리 효율성 극대화 및 정보 손실 최소화
**실제 적용의 계산 복잡도** / Computational Implementation Challenges: 이론적 우수성에도 불구하고 확률 과정 기반 계산의 높은 복잡도로 인한 실무 적용 시 제약

articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00036v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hippocampal place and time cells encode spatial and temporal aspects of experience. Both have the same neural substrate, but have been modeled as having different functions and mechanistic origins, place cells as continuous attractors, and time cells as leaky integrators. Here, we show that both types emerge from two dynamical regimes of a single recurrent network (RNN) modeling hippocampal CA3 as a predictive autoencoder. The network receives simulated, partially occluded ``experience vectors" containing spatial patterns (location-specific activ
articles.aiSummary:
The hippocampus, a crucial brain region for memory, contains place cells that encode spatial information and time cells that encode temporal information. Previously, these cells were thought to operate through different mechanisms, but this study demonstrates that both cell types may emerge from the same neural circuit. The research team modeled the hippocampal CA3 region using a recurrent neural network (RNN) functioning as a predictive autoencoder, processing partially occluded experience data containing spatial patterns. Results showed that both place cell and time cell characteristics spontaneously formed from two distinct dynamical regimes within the same network. This finding supports the understanding that the brain integrates spatial and temporal information in a unified manner, with significant implications for understanding fundamental memory formation mechanisms. However, the current model represents a simplified version that does not fully capture the complexity of actual brain function.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.titleKo: 인간-기계 인지 능력 향상을 위한 초음파 뇌-컴퓨터 인터페이스
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00349v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-intensity transcranial focused ultrasound (tFUS) is rapidly emerging as a transformative non-invasive brain stimulation (NIBS) modality characterized by high spatial resolution and ability to target deep brain circuits. Unlike electromagnetic techniques such as transcranial magnetic stimulation and transcranial direct current stimulation, which are constrained by centimeter-scale resolution and a depth-focality tradeoff, tFUS leverages mechanical pressure waves to modulate both superficial cortical and deep subcortical structures with millime
articles.aiSummary:
This research paper explores the potential of low-intensity transcranial focused ultrasound (tFUS) stimulation as a non-invasive brain stimulation (NIBS) modality with significant advantages over existing technologies. While conventional electromagnetic techniques such as transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) and transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) suffer from centimeter-scale resolution and depth-focality tradeoffs, tFUS employs mechanical pressure waves to selectively stimulate both superficial cortical and deep subcortical brain structures with millimeter-level precision. This technology presents revolutionary applications in the brain-computer interface (BCI) field and offers new possibilities for enhancing human cognitive abilities through neuroplasticity modulation. The study's key significance lies in achieving both non-invasiveness and high spatial resolution simultaneously—a feat previously unattainable with traditional methods. However, current limitations include insufficient long-term safety data, inadequate understanding of tFUS's biological mechanisms, and lack of established regulatory standards for clinical applications. With further research validating the safety and efficacy of this technology, tFUS could become a transformative tool for treating neurological disorders and enhancing cognitive performance.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.titleKo: OpenScope 프로그램을 통한 예측적 처리의 신경 메커니즘: 협력적 커뮤니티 실험
articles.summary: arXiv:2504.09614v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: This review synthesizes advances in predictive processing within the sensory cortex. Predictive processing theorizes that the brain continuously predicts sensory inputs, refining neuronal responses by highlighting prediction errors. We identify key computational primitives, such as stimulus adaptation, dendritic computation, excitatory/inhibitory balance and hierarchical processing, as central to this framework. Our review highlights convergences, such as top-down inputs and inhibitory interneurons shaping mismatch signals, and divergences, i
articles.aiSummary: This review article synthesizes advances in understanding predictive processing mechanisms in sensory cortex. Predictive processing theory posits that the brain continuously predicts incoming sensory inputs and refines neural responses by emphasizing prediction errors. The research team, working through the OpenScope collaborative program, identified key computational primitives including stimulus adaptation, dendritic computation, excitatory/inhibitory balance, and hierarchical processing as central to this framework. The study revealed convergences—such as top-down inputs and inhibitory interneurons shaping mismatch signals—while also documenting divergences in the field's understanding. This work is significant for understanding fundamental brain information processing principles and demonstrates the value of community-wide collaboration in neuroscience. However, the findings are limited by their reliance on animal models and focus on specific cortical regions.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 이 연구는 감각 피질에서 일어나는 예측처리(predictive processing) 메커니즘을 종합적으로 분석한 검토 논문입니다. 예측처리는 뇌가 지속적으로 들어오는 감각 정보를 예측하고, 예측 오류를 강조함으로써 신경 반응을 정교하게 조정한다는 이론입니다. 연구팀은 자극 적응(stimulus adaptation), 수상돌기 계산(dendritic computation), 흥분/억제 균형, 계층적 처리 등을 이 프레임워크의 핵심 계산 원리로 규명했습니다. OpenScope 프로그램을 통한 협력 연구로, 상향식 입력과 억제 신경세포가 불일치 신호를 형성하는 수렴점과 함께 상이한 점들도 도출했습니다. 이 연구는 뇌의 기본적인 정보처리 원리를 이해하는 데 중요하며, 신경과학 커뮤니티의 협력을 통해 진행되었다는 점이 의의입니다. 다만 동물모델과 제한된 피질 영역에 국한된 점이 제한사항입니다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: This review article synthesizes advances in understanding predictive processing mechanisms in sensory cortex. Predictive processing theory posits that the brain continuously predicts incoming sensory inputs and refines neural responses by emphasizing prediction errors. The research team, working through the OpenScope collaborative program, identified key computational primitives including stimulus adaptation, dendritic computation, excitatory/inhibitory balance, and hierarchical processing as central to this framework. The study revealed convergences—such as top-down inputs and inhibitory interneurons shaping mismatch signals—while also documenting divergences in the field's understanding. This work is significant for understanding fundamental brain information processing principles and demonstrates the value of community-wide collaboration in neuroscience. However, the findings are limited by their reliance on animal models and focus on specific cortical regions.
keyPoints: **예측처리 이론의 핵심**: 뇌는 수동적으로 감각을 받아들이지 않고 지속적으로 다음에 올 자극을 예측하며, 실제 자극과 예측 간의 오류를 감지하여 학습 / **Core of Predictive Processing Theory**: The brain actively predicts incoming sensory inputs rather than passively receiving them, and learns by detecting errors between predictions and actual stimuli

**네 가지 핵심 신경 메커니즘**: 자극 적응, 수상돌기 계산, 신경세포 간 흥/억 균형, 그리고 계층적인 정보처리가 예측처리의 기본 원리 / **Four Key Neural Mechanisms**: Stimulus adaptation, dendritic computation, excitatory/inhibitory balance, and hierarchical processing form the foundation of predictive processing

**상향식-하향식 상호작용**: 억제 신경세포와 상향식 입력이 협력하여 예측 오류(불일치 신호)를 형성하는 메커니즘 규명 / **Top-down and Bottom-up Interaction**: Identification of how inhibitory interneurons and top-down inputs collaborate to generate prediction error (mismatch) signals

**협력 연구의 중요성**: OpenScope 프로그램을 통한 개방형 협력이 신경과학 분야의 수렴된 이해를 도출하는 데 효과적임을 입증 / **Value of Collaborative Research**: The OpenScope program demonstrates the effectiveness of open collaboration in achieving consensus understanding across neuroscience community

articles.title: The psychological divide between Democrats and Republicans during democratic backsliding
articles.titleKo: 민주주의 후퇴 시기의 민주당과 공화당 간의 심리적 분열
articles.summary: A new study finds that people's happiness and life satisfaction are closely associated with their support for government policies. The findings highlight how political alignment relates to mental well-being during periods of rapid political change.
articles.aiSummary:
A recent study examines the psychological divide between Democratic and Republican supporters during periods of democratic backsliding. Researchers discovered a strong association between people's support for government policies and their happiness and life satisfaction levels. The study demonstrates how political alignment—the degree to which one's party holds power—affects mental well-being during times of rapid political change. The findings reveal that individuals' psychological well-being can vary significantly depending on whether their political views align with current government policies. The significance of this research lies in demonstrating that political system instability directly impacts citizens' psychological health, extending beyond merely political concerns. However, specific sample sizes, detailed methodologies, and precise numerical results are not fully provided in the available information, limiting generalizability and requiring further verification.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…a Lineman
articles.summary: Wiring a neighborhood back to life after a tornado, coveting the work of helicopter linemen in Faraday suits, and surviving the collapse of a rotten utility pole with Elden Rivas, a journeyman lineman in Houston, Texas. articles.aiSummary: This article features an episode from the podcast series "What It's Like to Be...", which explores the daily experiences of Elden Rivas, a journeyman lineman in Houston, Texas. Hosted by bestselling author Dan Heath, the series focuses on understanding the actual experiences and challenges of specific professions. The episode covers various aspects of linework, including post-tornado neighborhood power restoration efforts, specialized helicopter lineman operations using Faraday suits, and surviving a rotten utility pole collapse. The significance of this work lies in raising public awareness about the real working conditions and psychological challenges faced by workers in high-risk industries. However, as interview-based personal narratives, the content lacks scientific data and representative sampling. The podcast format allows listeners to gain deeper understanding of the lineman profession through firsthand accounts and authentic storytelling.

articles.title: Millions start work too early. This drug helps them stay awake
articles.titleKo: 수백만 명이 너무 일찍 일을 시작한다. 이 약물이 그들을 깨어있게 해준다
articles.summary: Millions of people start work before sunrise—but their brains aren’t ready for it. A new clinical trial has found that the wake-promoting drug solriamfetol can significantly boost alertness in early-morning shift workers struggling with shift work disorder. Participants who took the drug were able to stay awake and function better throughout full shifts, with improvements in productivity, safety, and daily performance.
articles.aiSummary:
Millions of people who start work before sunrise experience alertness problems because their circadian rhythms are not aligned with early work schedules. A recent clinical trial found that solriamfetol, a wake-promoting drug, significantly improves alertness in early-morning shift workers suffering from shift work disorder. Participants who received the drug were able to maintain wakefulness and perform better throughout their entire work shifts. The medication effectively improved workers' productivity, safety, and overall job performance. This study is significant as it offers a practical medical solution for millions of workers suffering from circadian rhythm disorders. However, additional validation is needed as the study lacks detailed information on participant numbers, dosage, side effects, and long-term outcomes.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
새벽 시간에 일을 시작하는 수백만 명의 근로자들은 생체리듬이 맞지 않아 각성 문제를 겪고 있다. 최근 임상시험에서 각성 촉진제인 솔리암페톨이 교대근무 수면장애를 앓는 조기 근무 근로자들의 주의력을 크게 향상시킬 수 있음을 발견했다. 연구에 참여한 약물 투여 그룹은 전체 근무 시간 동안 깨어있는 상태를 유지하고 더 나은 기능을 수행할 수 있었다. 이 약물은 근로자들의 생산성, 안전성, 일상적 업무 수행 능력을 개선하는 데 효과적이었다. 본 연구는 생체리듬 장애로 고통받는 수백만 근로자에게 실질적인 의료 솔루션을 제시한다는 점에서 의의가 있다. 다만 구체적인 참가자 수, 투여량, 부작용 등의 상세 데이터가 제시되지 않아 추가 검증이 필요하다.
